name: Dashlane
price_basis: Premium at $5.42 per month billed annually ($65.04 per year), checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KEEP: the failure mode is losing every account you own. $65 a year is cheap insurance.
what_zo_builds: Zo audits the subscription around the vault: it lists the devices and browsers you actually use, tracks whether the bundled VPN ever gets turned on, and files a reminder before renewal so the price gets a yearly review. It should not replace autofill, encrypted sync, or dark web monitoring with homemade scripts.
dont_kill_if: Keep it, or move to a cheaper manager with a deliberate, tested migration. The one wrong answer is exporting your vault to a spreadsheet on principle. If you never touch the VPN or dark web alerts, Bitwarden Premium does the core job for $19.80 a year.
build_brief: Goal: decide whether Dashlane Premium earns its renewal without weakening my account security. Inventory which Premium features I used in the last 90 days: VPN, dark web monitoring, secure sharing. Verify export works and restore a test entry into a second manager before any switch. Create a private note with the findings and an automation that reminds me 30 days before renewal. Never ingest vault contents into workspace files. Done when the renewal decision is written down and recovery is proven.
